# ThriveStack Product Telemetry вЂ” AI Instrumentation Guide
# Version: 3.2
# Hosted at: https://docs.thrivestack.ai/product/setup/llms.txt

---

## PURPOSE

This file gives AI coding assistants complete, executable instructions for instrumenting
any existing web app with ThriveStack product telemetry.
Every rule, schema, and example here is authoritative вЂ” follow exactly, do not deviate.

All event schemas are non-exhaustive. Additional properties beyond those listed are always allowed.

---

## HOW TO USE THIS GUIDE

Work through these phases in order. Do not write any code until Phase 0 is complete.

### Phase 0 вЂ” Understand the codebase first
0. Determine if the codebase is **frontend/full-stack** or **backend-only**:
   - If it runs in a browser or has a frontend entry point (Next.js, React, Vue, Nuxt, Angular, Svelte, plain HTML), continue with this guide.
   - If it is a **backend/server-side application with no browser context** (Node.js API server, Express, Fastify, Python/Django/FastAPI/Flask, Go service, Ruby on Rails API, etc.), **stop here** and use the backend guide instead:
     в†’ https://docs.thrivestack.ai/ai-setup/product/backend/llms.txt
1. Ask the developer: "Does your app have **accounts or organizations** that multiple users can belong to (B2B), or is each user their own independent account with no org/account entity (B2C)?"
   - **B2B** в†’ proceed with full group hierarchy search (STEP 2)
   - **B2C** в†’ skip hierarchy search, use B2C fallback directly (see STEP 2 вЂ” B2C fallback)
2. Identify the tech stack, auth library, and state management
3. Find `userId` вЂ” see STEP 2 for search patterns and rules
4. Find `groupId` вЂ” only if B2B; see STEP 2 for hierarchy, search patterns, and fallbacks
5. Find all instrumentable feature actions. Then **STOP** and ask the developer, exactly:
   > "I found these features: [list each one]. For each, which type applies?
   > - **core** вЂ” primary value driver, reason users signed up (e.g. Report builder, Pipeline view)
   > - **sticky** вЂ” habit-forming, pulls users back daily/weekly (e.g. Notifications, Workflow automation)
   > - **support** вЂ” switching cost builder, embeds product in operations (e.g. SSO, Billing, Audit logs)"
   Do not write any `feature_used` calls until the developer has answered for every feature.
6. **STOP** and ask the developer:
   > "Do you want to check for abusive or disposable email addresses during signup?
   > If yes вЂ” should ThriveStack (a) **block** the signup and show an error, or (b) **flag** the user for manual review and allow signup to proceed?"
   Do not write any `checkAbuseEmail` call until the developer has answered.

### Phase 1 вЂ” Install the script tag
Add once to the root layout. Follow STEP 1 exactly for the detected framework.

### Phase 2 вЂ” Instrument lifecycle events
Follow the FIRING ORDER section exactly for each flow found: signup, login, invite.

### Phase 3 вЂ” Instrument feature events
One `feature_used` event per confirmed feature action. `feature_name` in `[noun]_[verb]` snake_case.

### Phase 4 вЂ” Verify then summarise
Run through the VERIFICATION CHECKLIST. Fix anything missing. Then output the summary.
After the summary is printed, your task is complete. Do not continue. Do not fix unrelated issues, refactor surrounding code, or make any further edits.

**REQUIRED OUTPUT FORMAT вЂ” strictly follow this, no exceptions.**
Do NOT write a narrative summary. Do NOT output a "Summary of Changes" section. Do NOT describe what you did in prose.
Your only output after instrumentation is complete is the table below, printed inline in the chat. No files. No markdown files. No prose before or after it.

```
ThriveStack Instrumentation Summary
X/12 implemented

| # | Event | Status | Notes |
|---|-------|--------|-------|
| 0 | script tag URL | вњ…/вљ пёЏ/вќЊ | Exact src used |
| 1 | identify | вњ…/вљ пёЏ/вќЊ | Field used, file |
| 2 | group | вњ…/вљ пёЏ/вќЊ | Entity levels, billing root |
| 3 | signed_up | вњ…/вљ пёЏ/вќЊ | Handler, file |
| 4 | signed_in | вњ…/вљ пёЏ/вќЊ | Handler, file |
| 5 | account_created | вњ…/вљ пёЏ/вќЊ | Handler, file or N/A |
| 6 | account_added_user | вњ…/вљ пёЏ/вќЊ | Entry points covered |
| 7 | feature_used | вњ…/вљ пёЏ/вќЊ | Features and their feature_type |
| 8 | invite_sent | вњ…/вљ пёЏ/вќЊ | Handler, file or N/A |
| 9 | onboarding_step_completed | вњ…/вљ пёЏ/вќЊ | Steps found or N/A |
| 10 | signed_out | вњ…/вљ пёЏ/вќЊ | Handler, file |
| 11 | abuse_check | вњ…/вљ пёЏ/вќЊ | Signup file, block or flag |

User ID: [field] in [file]
Group hierarchy: [e.g. Organization в†’ Account (billing root) в†’ Workspace] or [B2C fallback]
Unresolved: [anything skipped and why, or "None"]

Instrumentation complete. Test your app as a real user вЂ” events will start flowing into ThriveStack.

Enabled Features:
- Departmental Analytics вЂ” X reports unlocked вЂ” insights across acquisition, activation, retention, and more.
  https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/home

---
Handy ThriveStack Resources
- Setup Progress: https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/onboarding/product-analytics/telemetry
- Diagnostics: https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/diagnostics-report
- API / Audit Logs: https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/events
- Developer Tools: https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/developer-tool
```

---

## STEP 1 вЂ” INSTALL THE SCRIPT TAG

This is the ONLY initialization method. There is no npm package. There is no init() call.

Add once to the root layout file. Never inside a component or page.

### Next.js App Router (app/layout.tsx)
```tsx
import Script from 'next/script'
export default function RootLayout({ children }) {
  return (
    &lt;html&gt;
      &lt;head&gt;
        &lt;Script
          src="https://ts-script.app.thrivestack.ai/latest/thrivestack.js"
          strategy="afterInteractive"
          data-api-key={process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_THRIVESTACK_API_KEY}
          data-source="product"
        /&gt;
      &lt;/head&gt;
      &lt;body&gt;{children}&lt;/body&gt;
    &lt;/html&gt;
  )
}
```

### React (public/index.html)
```html
&lt;script
  src="https://ts-script.app.thrivestack.ai/latest/thrivestack.js"
  api-key="%REACT_APP_THRIVESTACK_API_KEY%"
  source="product"&gt;
&lt;/script&gt;
```

### Vue / Nuxt / Plain HTML
```html
&lt;script
  src="https://ts-script.app.thrivestack.ai/latest/thrivestack.js"
  api-key="YOUR_ENV_VAR_HERE"
  source="product"&gt;
&lt;/script&gt;
```

Rules:
- Write the API key to the environment variable file appropriate for this stack (.env, .env.local, application.yml, etc.) вЂ” never in source code
- `api-key` must reference that environment variable вЂ” never hardcoded
- `source="product"` вЂ” always this exact string, never change it
- Global object after load: `thriveStack` вЂ” camelCase, lowercase 's'
- Place in root layout ONCE only

---

## STEP 2 вЂ” FIND userId AND groupId

### userId
Must be a stable unique identifier that does not change across sessions.
May be a UUID, integer, or any format the auth provider uses.
Prefer a stable unique ID over email. Email is acceptable as `user_id` if it is the only stable identifier available вЂ” use it without blocking.

Search patterns:
```javascript
const { user } = useAuth()               в†’ user.id
const session = useSession()             в†’ session.user.id
useContext(AuthContext)                  в†’ context.user.id
const { userId } = auth()               в†’ userId  (Clerk)
store.auth.user.id                       в†’ (Redux/Zustand)
claims.sub, claims.user_id              в†’ (JWT)
```

If no stable identifier found вЂ” stop and ask:
"What field uniquely identifies a user in your database вЂ” their ID, not their email?"

---

### groupId вЂ” Entity Hierarchy

Account is always mandatory вЂ” it is the billing/contractual root entity.
All other levels are additional and each gets its own `group()` call.

Default hierarchy:
```
Organization   (brand/company umbrella)
  в””в”Ђв”Ђ Account  в†ђ MANDATORY вЂ” billing root
        в””в”Ђв”Ђ Tenant
              в””в”Ђв”Ђ Workspace
                    в””в”Ђв”Ђ Team
```

This is a default вЂ” if the developer confirms a different entity is their billing root,
treat that as Account regardless of what it is called in code.

Search patterns:
```
AUTH CONTEXT:
  user.accountId / user.account.id
  user.orgId / user.organizationId / user.organization.id
  user.tenantId / user.workspaceId / user.companyId
  session.organization.id / session.account.id

JWT CUSTOM CLAIMS:
  claims.org_id / claims.account_id / claims.tenant_id
  claims['https://yourapp.com/org_id']   в†ђ Auth0 pattern

STORE (Redux/Zustand/Pinia):
  store.currentOrg.id / store.activeWorkspace.id
  useOrganization().organization.id

POST-LOGIN API RESPONSE:
  loginResponse.organization.id / meResponse.current_organization.id

URL PARAMS:
  /app/:accountId / /org/:orgId / /workspace/:workspaceId
  Note: if only in URL params, ask developer if always available
```

B2C / no-org fallback:
```javascript
group_id       = user.id         // same as userId
account_name   = user.name       // user's full name
account_domain = user.email      // full email address вЂ” not just domain
// omit plan_id and plan_name entirely
```
`group()` fires only once on first registration вЂ” never again.

Stop and ask if:
- No org-like field found after exhaustive search
- Multiple top-level entities exist and billing root is unclear
- groupId only in URL params and may not always be available

Never proceed without groupId. Never generate a fake ID.

### Context switching
When user switches active workspace/tenant/org:
- Do NOT re-fire `group()`
- Update `context.group_id` to the new entity's ID for all subsequent `track()` calls

---

## STEP 3 вЂ” identify()

Call on signup, login, and whenever new/updated user info is gathered.
`user_id` is always mandatory. Send whatever is known at the time.

```javascript
if (typeof thriveStack === 'undefined') return
thriveStack.identify([{
  "user_id": userId,
  "traits": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "user_name": user.name
  },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

---

## STEP 4 вЂ” group()

Fire when an entity is first created. One call per entity level found.
Never fire on login, context switch, or invite acceptance.

### Account (mandatory)
```javascript
if (typeof thriveStack === 'undefined') return
thriveStack.group([{
  "user_id": userId,
  "group_id": account.id,
  "traits": {
    "group_type": "Account",
    "account_domain": account.domain,
    "account_name": account.name,
    "plan_id": account.plan?.id,      // omit if unavailable
    "plan_name": account.plan?.name   // omit if unavailable
  },
  "context": {
    "group_id": account.id,           // must match group_id exactly
    "group_type": "Account"
  },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

### Additional levels (same signature, change group_type and ID)
```javascript
thriveStack.group([{
  "user_id": userId,
  "group_id": org.id,
  "traits": {
    "group_type": "Organization",     // "Organization"|"Tenant"|"Workspace"|"Team"
    "account_domain": org.domain,
    "account_name": org.name
  },
  "context": { "group_id": org.id, "group_type": "Organization" },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

`"Account"` is reserved for the billing root only.

---

## STEP 5 вЂ” TRACK EVENTS

### Base signature
```javascript
if (typeof thriveStack === 'undefined') return
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "event_name_here",
  "user_id": userId,
  "properties": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "user_name": user.name
  },
  "context": { "group_id": groupId },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

---

## EVENT SCHEMAS

### signed_up
When: New user registration completes.
Context: Include if groupId available. Omit if account doesn't exist yet.

```javascript
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "signed_up",
  "user_id": user.id,
  "properties": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "user_name": user.name,
    "utm_source": getUTMParam('utm_source'),
    "utm_medium": getUTMParam('utm_medium'),
    "utm_campaign": getUTMParam('utm_campaign'),
    "utm_term": getUTMParam('utm_term')
  },
  "context": { "group_id": user.orgId },   // omit if not yet available
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])

function getUTMParam(param) {
  return new URLSearchParams(window.location.search).get(param)
    || sessionStorage.getItem(param)
    || null
}
```

---

### signed_in
When: Existing user logs in successfully.
No UTM params. `context` is mandatory вЂ” groupId is always available at login.

```javascript
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "signed_in",
  "user_id": user.id,
  "properties": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "user_name": user.name,
    "user_role": user.role    // omit if unavailable
  },
  "context": { "group_id": groupId },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

---

### signed_out
When: Existing user logs out.
No UTM params. `context` is mandatory вЂ” groupId is available at logout.

```javascript
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "signed_out",
  "user_id": user.id,
  "properties": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "user_name": user.name,
    "user_role": user.role    // omit if unavailable
  },
  "context": { "group_id": groupId },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

---

### account_created
When: New account/org/workspace is first created.
`group()` must fire BEFORE this. Do not fire if joining an existing account.

```javascript
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "account_created",
  "user_id": user.id,
  "properties": {
    "account_id": org.id,
    "account_name": org.name,
    "account_domain": org.domain,
    "user_email": user.email,
    "user_name": user.name,
    "plan_name": org.plan?.name   // omit if unavailable
  },
  "context": { "group_id": org.id },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

---

### account_added_user
When: Account membership grows. Fire in ALL applicable entry points вЂ” missing any one is wrong.

Entry points:
1. New user direct signup + new account в†’ after `account_created` в†’ `join_method: "signup"`, `is_first_user: true`
2. New user invited to existing account в†’ after `signed_up` в†’ `join_method: "invite"`, `is_first_user: false`
3. Existing user invited, not yet in account в†’ after `signed_in` в†’ `join_method: "invite"`, `is_first_user: false`
4. SSO/SCIM/admin provisioning в†’ `join_method: "sso"` / `"scim"` / `"admin"`

Do NOT fire if user is already a member of the account.

```javascript
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "account_added_user",
  "user_id": user.id,
  "properties": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "user_name": user.name,
    "account_id": org.id,
    "account_name": org.name,
    "account_domain": org.domain,
    "role": user.role,
    "join_method": "signup",      // "signup"|"invite"|"sso"|"scim"|"admin"
    "is_first_user": true
  },
  "context": { "group_id": org.id },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

---

### onboarding_step_completed
When: User completes a discrete setup step.
Search for: `<Stepper>` `onStepComplete` `handleNext` `setCurrentStep` `markStepComplete`

```javascript
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "onboarding_step_completed",
  "user_id": user.id,
  "properties": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "user_name": user.name,
    "step_name": stepName,      // snake_case: "connected_data_source"
    "step_index": stepIndex,    // 0-based integer
    "total_steps": totalSteps
  },
  "context": { "group_id": org.id },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

---

### invite_sent
When: User sends a team invitation.
Search for: `inviteUser` `sendInvite` `inviteTeamMember` вЂ” forms with email + role fields.
All properties beyond `user_email` and `invitee_email` are send-if-available.

```javascript
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "invite_sent",
  "user_id": user.id,
  "properties": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "invitee_email": invitee.email,
    "invitee_role": invitee.role,
    "invitee_role_id": invitee.roleId,
    "invitee_user_id": invitee.userId,
    "invitee_team_id": invitee.teamId,
    "invitee_team": invitee.teamName,
    "feature_name": featureName,
    "sub_feature_name": subFeatureName,
    "source_url": window.location.href
  },
  "context": { "group_id": org.id },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

---

### feature_used
When: User performs any core product action.

- `event_name` is ALWAYS the literal string `"feature_used"` вЂ” never a custom verb
- `feature_name` is always `[noun]_[verb]` snake_case
- `feature_type` must be confirmed with developer before instrumenting вЂ” never assume

Ask the developer which applies:
- `"core"` вЂ” primary value driver, reason users signed up (e.g. Report builder, Pipeline view)
- `"sticky"` вЂ” habit-forming, pulls users back daily/weekly (e.g. Notifications, Workflow automation)
- `"support"` вЂ” switching cost builder, embeds product in operations (e.g. SSO, Billing, Audit logs)

```javascript
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "feature_used",
  "user_id": user.id,
  "properties": {
    "user_email": user.email,
    "user_name": user.name,
    "feature_name": "export_report",
    "feature_type": "core",
    "user_role": user.role    // omit if unavailable
  },
  "context": { "group_id": org.id },
  "timestamp": new Date().toISOString()
}])
```

---

### checkAbuseEmail вЂ” Abuse Prevention
When: Before signup form submission completes.
Prerequisites: ThriveStack SDK script tag installed (STEP 1).

Ask the developer: "Do you want to **block** signups from abusive emails, or **flag** them for review?"
Then implement the chosen path.

Place after the email input field and just before signup submission:

```javascript
if (typeof thriveStack === 'undefined') return
const checkAbuse = await thriveStack.checkAbuseEmail(userEmail)
if (checkAbuse.success && checkAbuse.data.isAbuse === 1) {
  // BLOCK path вЂ” prevent form submission and show error
  showError(checkAbuse.message)

  // FLAG path вЂ” allow signup but mark for review (use instead of block above)
  // flagUserForReview(userEmail, checkAbuse.data.reasonForAbuse)
}
```

Note: `await` is intentional here вЂ” this is a data check that gates the flow, not a fire-and-forget event.

`reasonForAbuse` fields: `isDisposable` (throwaway email provider), `domainExists` (false = domain not found), `isDuplicate` (email already seen in system).

---

## FIRING ORDER

### Direct signup вЂ” new account
```
identify в†’ group(Account) в†’ [group(Org/Workspace if present)] в†’ signed_up в†’ account_created в†’ account_added_user
```

### Direct signup вЂ” no new account (B2C or account pre-exists)
```
identify в†’ signed_up в†’ account_added_user
```

### Login
```
identify в†’ signed_in
```

### Logout
```
signed_out
```

### Invite вЂ” new user, existing account
```
identify в†’ signed_up в†’ account_added_user
```

### Invite вЂ” new user, new account
```
identify в†’ group(Account) в†’ signed_up в†’ account_created в†’ account_added_user
```

### Invite вЂ” existing user, not yet in account
```
identify в†’ signed_in в†’ account_added_user
```

### Invite вЂ” existing user, already in account
```
No events.
```

### SSO / SCIM / admin provisioning
```
identify в†’ account_added_user
```

---

## VERIFICATION CHECKLIST

Before delivering the summary, verify all 12 points. Fix anything missing first.

- [ ] 0. script tag вЂ” src is exactly `https://ts-script.app.thrivestack.ai/latest/thrivestack.js` вЂ” no variation, no substitution. Known wrong URLs to reject: any `cloudfront.net` URL, any `/v1/te.js` path, any URL that is not this exact string
- [ ] 1. identify вЂ” fires on signup, login, profile/info updates
- [ ] 2. group вЂ” fires on entity creation only, correct group_type per level
- [ ] 3. signed_up вЂ” correct flow, UTM captured
- [ ] 4. signed_in вЂ” fires on every login, context block present
- [ ] 5. account_created вЂ” only when new account actually created
- [ ] 6. account_added_user вЂ” all applicable entry points covered
- [ ] 7. feature_used вЂ” at least one, feature_type confirmed with developer
- [ ] 8. invite_sent вЂ” all invite handlers found
- [ ] 9. onboarding_step_completed вЂ” all stepper/wizard flows found
- [ ] 10. signed_out вЂ” fires on logout, context block present
- [ ] 11. abuse_check вЂ” checkAbuseEmail called before signup submission, block or flag implemented

### Summary format

Output this summary directly in the chat as your final message. Do not create any files for this summary. Do not write it to markdown. Print it inline.

**ThriveStack Instrumentation Summary**
X/12 implemented

| # | Event | Status | Notes |
|---|-------|--------|-------|
| 0 | script tag URL | вњ…/вљ пёЏ/вќЊ | Exact src used |
| 1 | identify | вњ…/вљ пёЏ/вќЊ | Field used, file |
| 2 | group | вњ…/вљ пёЏ/вќЊ | Entity levels, billing root |
| 3 | signed_up | вњ…/вљ пёЏ/вќЊ | Handler, file |
| 4 | signed_in | вњ…/вљ пёЏ/вќЊ | Handler, file |
| 5 | account_created | вњ…/вљ пёЏ/вќЊ | Handler, file or N/A |
| 6 | account_added_user | вњ…/вљ пёЏ/вќЊ | Entry points covered |
| 7 | feature_used | вњ…/вљ пёЏ/вќЊ | Features and their feature_type |
| 8 | invite_sent | вњ…/вљ пёЏ/вќЊ | Handler, file or N/A |
| 9 | onboarding_step_completed | вњ…/вљ пёЏ/вќЊ | Steps found or N/A |
| 10 | signed_out | вњ…/вљ пёЏ/вќЊ | Handler, file |
| 11 | abuse_check | вњ…/вљ пёЏ/вќЊ | Signup file, block or flag |

**User ID:** [field] in [file]
**Group hierarchy:** [e.g. Organization в†’ Account (billing root) в†’ Workspace] or [B2C fallback]
**Unresolved:** [anything skipped and why, or "None"]

Instrumentation complete. Test your app as a real user вЂ” events will start flowing into ThriveStack.

**Enabled Features:**
- Departmental Analytics вЂ” X reports unlocked вЂ” insights across acquisition, activation, retention, and more.
  https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/home

---

**Handy ThriveStack Resources**
- Setup Progress: https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/onboarding/product-analytics/telemetry
- Diagnostics: https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/diagnostics-report
- API / Audit Logs: https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/events
- Developer Tools: https://app.thrivestack.ai/analyze/developer-tool

---

## HARD RULES

1. Script src must be exactly `https://ts-script.app.thrivestack.ai/latest/thrivestack.js` вЂ” never alter the URL. Known wrong substitutions to reject: any `cloudfront.net` URL, any `/v1/te.js` path, any URL that is not this exact string
2. Global object: `thriveStack` вЂ” never `ThriveStack` or `thrivestack`
3. Every method takes an array: `identify([{...}])` `group([{...}])` `track([{...}])`
4. Guard every call: `if (typeof thriveStack === 'undefined') return`
5. timestamp: `new Date().toISOString()` on every call вЂ” when multiple events fire in the same handler, use `const now = Date.now()` once and offset each by 1ms: `new Date(now).toISOString()`, `new Date(now + 1).toISOString()`, etc.
6. `group_type: "Account"` reserved for billing root only
7. `context.group_id` must equal `group_id` on group() вЂ” same variable always
8. `context` required on every `track()` вЂ” except `signed_up` when groupId not yet available
9. userId and groupId from auth context only вЂ” never hardcode, never generate
10. `api-key` from environment variable only вЂ” never in source code
11. Fire-and-forget вЂ” never `await` thriveStack calls
12. Fire after action succeeds вЂ” inside `.then()` or after `await`
13. `group()` on entity creation only вЂ” never on login or context switch
14. `feature_used` is always the literal event_name вЂ” never a custom verb
15. `feature_type` confirmed with developer вЂ” never assumed. Never write a `feature_used` call without an explicit answer from the developer for that feature's type. Skipping this question is not allowed.
15a. Abuse check question is mandatory вЂ” never skip it. Never implement `checkAbuseEmail` without first asking the developer whether they want block or flag. Never assume block. Never assume flag.
16. `account_added_user` fires in all entry points вЂ” signup, invite, SSO/SCIM/admin
17. Do not fire `account_added_user` if user is already a member
18. `user_added_to_account` does not exist вЂ” use `account_added_user`
19. Additional properties beyond documented ones are always permitted
20. Stop after the summary is output вЂ” do not fix bugs, refactor code, clean up existing logic, or make any other changes outside of ThriveStack instrumentation. Your only permitted modifications are the ThriveStack calls added in this guide.

---

## CRITICAL MISTAKES

WRONG вЂ” missing array:
```javascript
thriveStack.track({ "event_name": "signed_up" })
```
RIGHT:
```javascript
thriveStack.track([{ "event_name": "signed_up" }])
```

WRONG вЂ” no guard:
```javascript
thriveStack.identify([{ "user_id": user.id }])
```
RIGHT:
```javascript
if (typeof thriveStack === 'undefined') return
thriveStack.identify([{ "user_id": user.id }])
```

WRONG вЂ” group() on login:
```javascript
async function handleLogin() {
  const { user, org } = await auth.signIn(...)
  thriveStack.group([{ "group_id": org.id }])
}
```
RIGHT:
```javascript
async function handleLogin() {
  const { user, org } = await auth.signIn(...)
  thriveStack.identify([{ "user_id": user.id, ... }])
  thriveStack.track([{ "event_name": "signed_in", ... }])
}
```

WRONG вЂ” custom event_name for feature:
```javascript
thriveStack.track([{ "event_name": "report_exported" }])
```
RIGHT:
```javascript
thriveStack.track([{
  "event_name": "feature_used",
  "properties": { "feature_name": "report_exported", "feature_type": "core" }
}])
```

WRONG вЂ” generating groupId:
```javascript
thriveStack.group([{ "group_id": uuidv4() }])
```
RIGHT:
```javascript
thriveStack.group([{ "group_id": user.orgId }])
```

WRONG вЂ” context.group_id mismatch:
```javascript
thriveStack.group([{
  "group_id": org.id,
  "context": { "group_id": uuidv4() }
}])
```
RIGHT:
```javascript
thriveStack.group([{
  "group_id": org.id,
  "context": { "group_id": org.id }
}])
```
